A small '''battle''' occurred in the [[Morobe system]] above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Talasea]] in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] between [[Wraith Squadron]] and elements of [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]'s fleet.

A task force led by the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Implacable (Star Destroyer)/Legends|''Implacable'']], under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Apwar Trigit]], was sent to [[Talasea]] in the [[Morobe system]], where information from [[Project Morrt]] indicated that the survivors of Trigit's failed assault on [[Folor]] had congregated. However, Project Morrt had been compromised by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and a large fleet actually awaited Trigit in an ambush. Intelligence analyst [[Gara Petothel]], about the ''Implacable'', realized the deception, but not until after the task force had jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Unable to contact his support ships, Trigit was forced to drop out of hyperspace prematurely and was unable to warn them of the trap.

As a result, the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Provocateur]]'' and the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[Constrictor]]'' arrived in the Morobe system without the Star Destroyer. Their companion, the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] [[Ession Strike|''Night Caller'']], had actually been captured by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] Wraith Squadron, and immediately turned on Zsinj's ships. Its first target was the communication arrays of the enemy ships, to prevent them from revealing that the ''Night Caller'' was now under New Republic control. Wraith pilot [[Garik Loran|Garik "Face" Loran]], on the bridge in case he was called upon to impersonate the ''Night Caller''{{'s}} deceased captain [[Zurel Darillian]], suggested that the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] in the ship's forward hold launch a salvo of [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] against the ''Provocateur'' before launching from the forward hangar, a tactic that would come to be known as the "[[Loran Spitball]]." The other Wraith pilots then launched into combat, but before Face could reach his fighter, it and the ship's bridge were both destroyed by a [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] volley from the ''Provocateur''. Loran took command of the ship from its secondary bridge, while the Wraiths' other X-wings attacked the frigate and their [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] attacked the enemy corvette. [[Kell Tainer]] organized the fighters into a massed proton torpedo attack which split the ''Provocateur'' in half along its central spar, venting its atmosphere. The heavily damaged ''Constrictor'' then surrendered to the New Republic forces.

A large New Republic force, led by the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']] and including [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], had been waiting at [[Talasea]] for the ''Implacable'', but they played no role in the battle. The New Republic ships could not reach the ''Provocateur'' in time to rescue any of its crew, and the ship was considered unsalvageable. The secret of the ''Night Caller''{{'s}} capture was maintained, allowing Wraith Squadron to continue their deception and set [[Battle of Ession|another trap]] for the ''Implacable''.

==Behind the scenes==
The battle of Talasea first appeared in ''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]'', the fifth volume of the [[Star Wars: X-Wing (novel series)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'' novel series]], authored by [[Aaron Allston]] and published in [[1998]].<ref name="Wraith" /> It shares its name with the real-world [[Wikipedia:Battle of Talasea|Battle of Talasea]], a skirmish in [[Wikipedia:New Guinea|New Guinea]] during [[Wikipedia:World War II|World War II]] between the [[Wikipedia:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]] and combined [[Wikipedia:Australia|Australian]] and [[Wikipedia:United States|American]] forces.
